task.summary: Initial commit. Add basic routing and templates
Initial commit. Add basic routing and templates
Added templates structure. Navbar, main content, sidebar for updates. Buttons for register and login. Render dynamic dummy content. Added some basic .css styling.

Create a DB and structure the app to act as a package. Models.py creates classes for User, Post table. Create a SQLAlchemy DB locally: site.db. Refactor the app to have a Package structure: __init__.py. Split the logic to specific files.

Create an authentication system using flask-bcrypt and flask-login extensions. The user can log in and log out from application. Save the User data to DB. Create hashed passwords for secure storage. Make sure the User don't already exist in DB by verifying the username and email data - in RegistrationForms. Add logout and account routes. Create an 'Account' template. Check if the current user is authenticated and add restrictions. Change the right side navigation if it has a current user authenticated to show 'Login' and 'Account' options.

Update Account Info Populate fields with user's info. Update the user info and save it to DB. Profile picture - have a default image. User can set a custom picture. The picture is saved as a random hex named image file (using secrets package). The valid format accepted are JPG and PNG. The image is resized (using Pillow package) and saved to the path /static/profile_images.

Create, Update and Delete blog posts. Build a user interface for creating posts and updating the existing ones, including input validation. Implement user-specific post pages. Delete posts using a Bootstrap Modal.

Some pagination Add page interation for showing posts (show th number of 3 posts). Sort posts based on the newest ones. Add a new template for user posts. Navigation by click-ing the post's author name. Create a new route to filter the posts by author and list them.

Reset Password Via Email Use Flask-mail and itsdangerous packages. Add reset password request templates, forms and routes. Generate reset tokens with itsdangerous pkg that expires. Use Flask-mail to configure a gmail server. Generate an APP PASSW0RD from Google in order to be able to authenticate third-party apps (myaccount.google.com/apppasswords). Send a message on email with a link to reset_token template. Save the user's new password on DB. Add an .env file for credentials.
Restructure the application to use Blueprints. Split up the application into more manageable sections.
Application Factory Pattern Wrap the application creation inside a function (create_app()).

MESSAGE.value: Initial commit. Add basic routing and templates
Added templates structure. Navbar, main content, sidebar for updates. Buttons for register and login. Render dynamic dummy content. Added some basic .css styling.

Create a DB and structure the app to act as a package. Models.py creates classes for User, Post table. Create a SQLAlchemy DB locally: site.db. Refactor the app to have a Package structure: __init__.py. Split the logic to specific files.

Create an authentication system using flask-bcrypt and flask-login extensions. The user can log in and log out from application. Save the User data to DB. Create hashed passwords for secure storage. Make sure the User don't already exist in DB by verifying the username and email data - in RegistrationForms. Add logout and account routes. Create an 'Account' template. Check if the current user is authenticated and add restrictions. Change the right side navigation if it has a current user authenticated to show 'Login' and 'Account' options.

Update Account Info Populate fields with user's info. Update the user info and save it to DB. Profile picture - have a default image. User can set a custom picture. The picture is saved as a random hex named image file (using secrets package). The valid format accepted are JPG and PNG. The image is resized (using Pillow package) and saved to the path /static/profile_images.

Create, Update and Delete blog posts. Build a user interface for creating posts and updating the existing ones, including input validation. Implement user-specific post pages. Delete posts using a Bootstrap Modal.

Some pagination Add page interation for showing posts (show th number of 3 posts). Sort posts based on the newest ones. Add a new template for user posts. Navigation by click-ing the post's author name. Create a new route to filter the posts by author and list them.

Reset Password Via Email Use Flask-mail and itsdangerous packages. Add reset password request templates, forms and routes. Generate reset tokens with itsdangerous pkg that expires. Use Flask-mail to configure a gmail server. Generate an APP PASSW0RD from Google in order to be able to authenticate third-party apps (myaccount.google.com/apppasswords). Send a message on email with a link to reset_token template. Save the user's new password on DB. Add an .env file for credentials.
Restructure the application to use Blueprints. Split up the application into more manageable sections.
Application Factory Pattern Wrap the application creation inside a function (create_app()).
